name: Source Fidelity review for Resolution Pathway And Tissue Repair bundle
summary: The staged source, study, finding, intervention, and outlook records match the cited PubMed/E-utilities and ClinicalTrials.gov facts closely enough for publication review. The bundle preserves the critical source boundaries: aged-mouse RvD1/MaR1 repair data are not treated as human efficacy, human fish-oil/SPM records are biomarker and target-engagement evidence, EPA+DHA venous-ulcer studies remain disease-adjacent repair evidence, and completed registry entries without posted results are kept as watch items rather than efficacy claims.

findings.claim_or_issue: The completed older-adult EPA+DHA venous-ulcer trial is used only as a registry watch item because the current ClinicalTrials.gov API view shows NCT03576989 as completed with hasResults=false.
findings.why_it_matters: Registry status can change over time, and completion without posted results cannot support either positive or negative efficacy interpretation.
findings.recommended_action: No revision required. Keep this finding framed as a no-results surveillance boundary and recheck the registry during later surveillance before upgrading or downgrading the track.

findings.claim_or_issue: Several human records test EPA+DHA, fish-oil, SPM-enriched marine oil, or SPM Active products rather than direct RvD1 or MaR1 administration.
findings.why_it_matters: Those sources support target engagement and lipid-mediator changes, but not direct specialized pro-resolving mediator efficacy in humans.
findings.recommended_action: No revision required. Preserve the staged intervention and finding caveats that separate precursor or enriched-oil supplementation from direct SPM repair claims.

findings.claim_or_issue: The chronic venous leg ulcer evidence is wound-disease-adjacent and biomarker-heavy, with wound-area trends from a small pilot study.
findings.why_it_matters: The source supports a repair-relevant signal, but broad healthy-aging tissue-repair claims would overstate source fidelity.
findings.recommended_action: No revision required. Keep the finding in the human_biomarker tier and maintain the disease-adjacent wound-care caveats.

findings.claim_or_issue: The EPA+DHA wound-fluid PMN finding is backed by DOI 10.1111/wrr.12558 rather than a staged PubMed source record.
findings.why_it_matters: This is acceptable when the DOI record is complete, but reviewers should not infer missing provenance from the absence of a PMID-form source ID.
findings.recommended_action: No revision required. Keep the DOI source record and associated study/finding linkage as staged.
